signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation at 2.2% | NO | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate rose to 2.2% in March 2026, up from 2.0% in prior months but below market forecasts of 2.4%; this recent confirmation indicates contained price pressures despite energy volatility, unlikely to significantly shift prediction markets given alignment with central bank targets and prior stability.[1

signals.headline: South Korea Q4 2025 GDP Contraction Revised Milder | NO | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
South Korea Q4 2025 GDP Contraction Revised Milder | NO | MED confidence**  
South Korea's GDP contraction in Q4 2025 was revised to a slightly slower pace than initially estimated, due to weaker investment and exports amid oil supply shocks; while impactful on growth outlook, this pertains to last year's data (reported March 2026) and follows Q3 stimulus, limiting fresh market-moving implications
